title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment and install all required packages for SAE analysis, attribution patching, and steering evaluation.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.3+ with CUDA: pip install torch torchvision --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step4: Install SAELens for pretrained SAEs: pip install sae-lens>=2.0.0
steps.step5: Install datasets and evaluation libraries: pip install datasets>=2.14.0 evaluate>=0.4.0 scikit-learn scipy
steps.step6: Install visualization: pip install matplotlib seaborn plotly
steps.step7: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'

title: Download Models and Pretrained SAEs
description: Load language models and associated pretrained SAEs from SAELens. Focus on GPT-2 Small for primary experiments due to compute constraints.

steps.step1: Download GPT-2 Small (124M) via TransformerLens: from transformer_lens import HookedTransformer; model = HookedTransformer.from_pretrained('gpt2-small')
steps.step2: Load pretrained SAEs from SAELens for GPT-2 Small layer 8 (resid_post): from sae_lens import SAE; sae, cfg_dict, sparsity = SAE.from_pretrained(release='gpt2-small-res-jb', sae_id='blocks.8.hook_resid_pre', device='cuda')
steps.step3: Load additional SAEs with different MSE/sparsity tradeoffs: sae_id='blocks.8.hook_resid_pre' with different checkpoints if available
steps.step4: Download Pythia-160M SAE for scaling analysis: SAE.from_pretrained(release='pythia-160m-res-jb', sae_id='blocks.8.hook_resid_pre')
steps.step5: Verify SAE configurations: print(cfg_dict) to confirm d_model=768, d_sae=24576 for GPT-2
steps.step6: Cache models to local directory to avoid repeated downloads

title: Prepare Behavioral Evaluation Datasets
description: Download and preprocess datasets for measuring target behaviors and side effects.

steps.step1: Load TruthfulQA (Lin et al., 2022) for truthfulness evaluation: from datasets import load_dataset; tqa = load_dataset('truthful_qa', 'generation', split='validation')
steps.step2: Load BBQ (Parrish et al., 2022) for bias evaluation: bbq = load_dataset('heegyu/bbq', 'default', split='test')
steps.step3: Create contrastive prompt pairs for each behavior: e.g., 'The capital of France is Paris' (true) vs 'The capital of France is London' (false)
steps.step4: Generate 500 contrastive prompt pairs per behavior (truthfulness, bias, sentiment): Store as list of dicts with 'positive', 'negative', 'neutral' variants
steps.step5: Load general capability datasets: hellaswag = load_dataset('hellaswag', split='validation[:500]') for side effect measurement
steps.step6: Create data splits: 80% for IFS computation, 20% held-out for final steering evaluation
steps.step7: Cache preprocessed datasets to disk as JSON files

title: Create Synthetic Ground-Truth Validation Set
description: Generate synthetic dataset with known causal features to validate attribution patching accuracy.

steps.step1: Generate 100 simple arithmetic prompts: 'What is X + Y?' where X, Y in range(1, 10)
steps.step2: Create templates with clear patterns: 'The answer to {X} plus {Y} equals'
steps.step3: Store ground truth expected outputs for validation
steps.step4: Create neutral context prompts without arithmetic for necessity measurement

title: Implement Baseline 1: Random Feature Selection
description: Simple baseline following He et al. (2025) - steering with random SAE features to establish minimum performance threshold.

steps.step1: Implement random feature selection: selected_features = random.sample(range(d_sae), k=num_features)
steps.step2: Implement steering function: steered_output = model.generate(input_ids, intervention=add_feature_activation(features, coefficient=20))
steps.step4: Evaluate on TruthfulQA: measure truthfulness score change before/after steering
steps.step5: Measure side effects on HellaSwag: compute capability preservation score
steps.step6: Run for k in [10, 20, 50, 100] features to study scaling

title: Implement Baseline 2: Activation-Based Steering (Templeton et al., 2024)
description: Strong baseline - standard SAE steering that selects features with highest mean activation on target prompts.

steps.step1: Compute mean activation for each feature on target behavior prompts: acts = sae.encode(model(target_prompts))
steps.step2: Rank features by mean activation: feature_scores = acts.mean(dim=0)
steps.step3: Select top-k features by activation magnitude
steps.step4: Apply steering with coefficient 20 (standard value from Templeton et al.)
steps.step5: Evaluate target behavior change on TruthfulQA, BBQ
steps.step6: Measure side effects on HellaSwag general capability
steps.step7: Run for k in [10, 20, 50, 100] and seeds [42, 43, 44]

title: Implement Baseline 3: Output Score Filtering (Arad et al., 2025)
description: Most closely related work - filter features by output score (sufficiency) before steering, as described in EMNLP 2025 paper.

steps.step1: Implement output score computation per Arad et al.: For each feature f, measure logit difference when f is activated
steps.step2: Compute sufficiency score: Suf(f) = E[KL(output_dist | f=alpha) || output_dist | f=0)] on neutral prompts
steps.step3: Filter features: keep only features with output_score > threshold (0.01 as per Arad et al.)
steps.step4: From filtered set, select top-k by activation (Arad et al. method)
steps.step5: Apply steering with same coefficient as other baselines
steps.step6: Evaluate on same metrics as other baselines
steps.step7: Run for k in [10, 20, 50, 100] and seeds [42, 43, 44]
steps.step9: This baseline directly compares against Arad et al.'s sufficiency-only approach

title: Compute Intervention Fidelity Score (IFS) Components
description: Compute necessity, sufficiency, and consistency scores for all SAE features using attribution patching for efficiency.

steps.step1: Implement necessity score: Nec(f) = E[P(y_target|x) - P(y_target|x, f=0)] using attribution patching to approximate ablation effect
steps.step2: Implement sufficiency score: Suf(f) = E[P(y_target|x, f=alpha) - P(y_target|x)] on neutral contexts (matches Arad et al. output score)
steps.step3: Implement consistency score: Cons(f) = 1 - Var[Delta_P(y_target|x, f)] / max_variance across contexts
steps.step4: Combine into IFS: IFS(f) = sqrt(Nec(f) * Suf(f)) * Cons(f)
steps.step5: Use attribution patching (Syed et al., 2024) for efficiency: compute gradients w.r.t feature activations
steps.step6: Process 500 contrastive prompt pairs for each behavior (truthfulness, bias)
steps.step7: Compute scores for all ~24,576 features in GPT-2 Small SAE
steps.step8: Save IFS scores to results/ifs_scores.json with structure: {feature_id: {nec, suf, cons, ifs, behavior}}

title: Validate Attribution Patching Against Direct Activation Patching
description: Verify that attribution patching approximations are accurate by comparing to direct activation patching on 10% of features.

steps.step1: Randomly sample 10% of features (n=2458) for validation
steps.step2: For sampled features, compute necessity and sufficiency using direct activation patching (slow but accurate)
steps.step3: Compare attribution patching estimates to direct patching results
steps.step4: Compute Spearman correlation between attribution and direct patching scores
steps.step5: If correlation < 0.8, identify problem features and adjust method
steps.step6: Report validation results: correlation coefficients and error distributions
steps.step8: Acknowledge limitations per Kramár et al. (2024) in final analysis

title: Implement Fidelity-Weighted Steering (FWS)
description: Our main method: select and weight features by full IFS rather than activation or output score alone.

steps.step1: Compute IFS for all features on target behavior (from previous step)
steps.step2: Rank features by IFS: sorted_features = sorted(features, key=lambda f: f.ifs, reverse=True)
steps.step4: Apply fidelity-weighted steering: coefficient * (IFS(f)^beta) where beta=0.5
steps.step5: Evaluate target behavior change on TruthfulQA and BBQ
steps.step7: Run for k in [10, 20, 50, 100] and seeds [42, 43, 44]

title: Component Ablation: Test Individual IFS Components
description: Quantify marginal contribution of necessity, sufficiency, and consistency by testing each component alone and in combination.

steps.step1: Sufficiency-only steering: Use only Suf(f) for selection (equivalent to output score baseline)
steps.step2: Necessity-only steering: Use only Nec(f) for selection
steps.step3: Consistency-only steering: Use only Cons(f) for selection
steps.step4: Sufficiency + Necessity: Use sqrt(Nec * Suf) without consistency
steps.step5: Sufficiency + Consistency: Use Suf * Cons without necessity
steps.step6: Full IFS: Use sqrt(Nec * Suf) * Cons
steps.step7: Evaluate all 6 variants on truthfulness steering with k=20 features
steps.step8: Report which components matter most for different behavior types

title: Feature Count Ablation
description: Study the tradeoff between number of features used and steering effectiveness.

steps.step2: For each k, evaluate FWS and all baselines
steps.step3: Plot efficiency curve: target behavior change vs. k

title: Correlation Analysis: Metrics vs. IFS
description: Measure correlation between traditional SAE metrics, output scores, and IFS to quantify the causal-semantic disconnect.

steps.step1: Compute traditional SAE metrics for each feature: mean activation, L0 norm contribution, reconstruction MSE contribution
steps.step2: Compute output scores (Arad et al.) for all features
steps.step3: Compute IFS components for all features
steps.step4: Calculate Spearman correlation between all metric pairs
steps.step5: Compute Precision@k: fraction of top-k features by metric A in top-k by IFS
steps.step6: Identify features with high traditional scores but low IFS (the 'disconnect')
steps.step7: Compare predictive power: can traditional metrics or output scores predict IFS?
steps.step8: Save correlation matrix and analysis to results/correlation_analysis.json

title: Scaling Analysis Across Model Sizes
description: Repeat key experiments on Pythia-160M and Pythia-410M to understand how results scale.

steps.step2: Compute IFS for all features (subset due to time constraints)
steps.step3: Run steering comparison: FWS vs. activation baseline vs. output score baseline
steps.step4: Measure: fraction of high-IFS features, correlation trends
steps.step5: If time permits, repeat with Pythia-410M

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Test whether observed differences between methods are statistically significant.

steps.step1: Load all results from baselines and main experiments
steps.step2: Perform paired t-tests between FWS and each baseline
steps.step3: Correct for multiple comparisons using Bonferroni
steps.step4: Compute confidence intervals for all metrics
steps.step6: Save statistical test results to results/statistical_tests.json

title: Evaluate Against Success Criteria
description: Check whether experimental results meet the predefined success criteria.

steps.step1: SC1: Check if IFS shows stronger correlation with steering effectiveness than output scores (Spearman ρ improvement ≥ 0.1)
steps.step2: SC2: Check if FWS achieves ≥90% of target behavior change with ≤50% as many features as activation baseline
steps.step3: SC3: Check if IFS-guided steering has ≤50% side effects of activation baseline and ≤70% of output-score baseline
steps.step4: SC4: Check if component ablation shows necessity/consistency provide improvements over sufficiency alone
steps.step5: Document which criteria are met, which are not, and by what margin
steps.step6: If criteria not met, analyze why and document limitations

title: Generate Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper.

steps.step1: Figure 1: Correlation heatmap between SAE metrics (reconstruction, L0, output score, IFS components)
steps.step2: Figure 2: Steering effectiveness comparison (bar chart: FWS vs. baselines on target behavior change)
steps.step3: Figure 3: Side effects comparison (bar chart: capability preservation for each method)
steps.step4: Figure 4: Component ablation results (grouped bar chart showing each component combination)
steps.step5: Figure 5: Feature efficiency curves (line plot: behavior change vs. number of features for each method)
steps.step6: Figure 6: IFS score distribution histogram with quartile annotations
steps.step7: Save all figures to figures/ directory as PNG and PDF
steps.step8: Generate summary statistics table for paper

title: Create Results Summary Tables
description: Generate LaTeX and markdown tables summarizing all experimental results.

steps.step1: Table 1: Main results comparing all methods on target behavior and side effects
steps.step5: Include mean ± std across 3 random seeds for all metrics
steps.step6: Save tables to tables/ directory as LaTeX and CSV
